channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick Says "Woe Is Me" AI Narrative Is Overstated
item.content:encoded:
Strauss Zelnick, the CEO of (among other IPs) Grand Theft Auto owner Take-Two, has given more of his thoughts on generative AI, and while he acknowledges the tech has potential risks, he says it will likely be "a great thing for humanity".

Speaking at an economic forum last week (thanks, GameSpot), Zelnick said he thinks people "spend too much time" discussing what he refers to as "the 'woe is me' risk related to AI", and that those who are worried about AI's potential impact on the world "might as well embrace it" since it's "going to come along anyhow".

Zelnick said he believes that AI will "undoubtedly be a good thing" for his company, and that it won't be replacing artists, but will instead allow them to do "higher-quality work", calling the viewpoint that AI leads to layoffs "the mistake of AI" (despite evidence to the contrary).

Zelnick went on to say that because AI is trained on data that already exists, humans will always be required to make new content. His view, which some might say is a little simplistic, is that "technology creates productivity, [then] productivity creates GDP, [and] GDP creates more employment".

Perhaps tellingly, Zelnick points to Elon Musk as an example of why AI won't take people's jobs (even though it already has), suggesting that the world's richest man has "an unlimited number of ideas" and that he "works 20 hours a day". Hmm.

Generative AI is proving to be an extremely divisive issue across the gaming industry. While companies like Take-Two, PUBG's Krafton, and EA have embraced it, others, including Manor Lords publisher Hooded Horse, have been vocal in their opposition.

Zelnick's comments come as Take-Two gears up to release Grand Theft Auto 6 in November. Needless to say, it's one of the most hotly-anticipated releases on the calendar, causing Hollow Knight: Silksong-style apprehension across the industry with regards to competing release dates.

Whether or not GTA 6 extensively uses generative AI remains to be seen, but it looks like Take-Two is happy to incorporate the technology into its workflow. We'll have to wait and see whether history bears that out as a good idea or not.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/take-two-strauss-zelnick-ai-comments
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Media Giant Toei Reveals New Gaming Brand Toei Games
item.content:encoded:
Media giant Toei Company, the force behind big IPs like Power Rangers and Kamen Rider (which you might know as Masked Rider depending on where you live), has announced a brand new gaming label by the name of Toei Games.

The announcement (for which we're using machine translation, as well as additional reporting by Gematsu) makes Toei today's second Japanese media company to announce that it's branching out into games. 

Unlike Sanrio, however, Toei says it intends to create titles that aren't based on its most iconic IPs, but instead "completely new game titles created by talented creators from Japan and abroad". Said games will be based on completely new properties as well.

We don't yet know what the first game from Toei Games will be, but we do know that it'll focus on the PC market and that more information about it will become available on April 24th. Mark that date in your calendar if you want to keep up with this one.

Toei CEO Fumio Yoshimura says the company wants gaming to become one of its core pillars alongside its existing exports, which include animation, movies, and live-action TV shows, among other things.

He says it's all part of the Toei New Wave 2033 strategy, which will see the company delivering its "stories" to "the entire world" (again, we're using machine translation, so there may be some slight inaccuracies in the wording of the quote).

The new Toei Games logo, which you can see at the top of this page, is a pixel art version of the company's iconic "waves on the shore" sequence, and it was designed by Game Dev Story studio Kairosoft.

We'll get to learn more about what Toei Games intends to do for its first project later this week. Remember, it won't be based on IPs like Kamen Rider or Super Sentai, so don't get your hopes up, eh?

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/toei-games-revealed
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hello Kitty Company Sanrio Announces Gaming Brand and New Party Game
item.content:encoded:
Sanrio, the company that owns the IP for the popular Hello Kitty brand, has announced the formation of its own gaming brand Sanrio Games, with a number of new projects set to launch over the next few years.

According to an Automaton Media report, Sanrio Games will launch ten games between now and March 2029, the first of which will be the appropriately-named party game Sanrio Party Land, which is set to arrive this fall.

Sanrio Party Land sounds like it'll play a lot like Mario Party; you'll create an avatar and play minigames with iconic Sanrio characters, so expect a Hello Kitty slant on Nintendo's popular party game franchise rather than the more cozy stylings of something like Hello Kitty Island Adventure.

Automaton says that another console game will be arriving at some point in the current fiscal year, which ends in March 2027, and that more info about each of Sanrio's upcoming projects will be announced "gradually".

Sanrio is best-known for its Hello Kitty brand, but that's not the only IP the company owns; it's also known for characters like Kuromi and Cinnamoroll, and it also owns the worldwide publishing rights for the Mr. Men franchise, as well as the Japanese publishing rights for the Charlie Brown-starring Peanuts.

Presumably, Hello Kitty herself will be featuring in many of Sanrio's upcoming efforts, and it won't be her first foray into the gaming world either. Many Hello Kitty games have been released as Japan-only ventures, alongside efforts like the aforementioned Island Adventure, which was created by a Western studio.

The character is also set to feature in a number of upcoming tabletop projects; back in 2023, Maestro Media signed a multi-year deal with Sanrio to create board game experiences based on Hello Kitty.

We'll have to wait and see what Sanrio has in store for its new gaming brand, but for now, you can look forward to Sanrio Party Land arriving later this year.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/sanrio-games-new-label
